subject: GeneralKnowledge
title: GeneralKnowledge Test - 02
description: A mix of fun and educational questions covering animals, geography, space, technology, and health for Grade 4 students.

questions.q: Which of these animals is known for having a long trunk?
questions.explanation: Elephants use their trunks for breathing, smelling, touching, grasping, and producing sound.
questions.hint: It is the largest land animal.
questions.objective: Identify common animals and their physical features.

questions.q: What is the capital city of Japan?
questions.explanation: Tokyo is the capital city of Japan and one of the most populated cities in the world.
questions.hint: It is a major island nation in East Asia.

questions.q: Which invention helped people communicate over long distances by voice?
questions.explanation: The telephone allows people to transmit and receive sound or voice across long distances.
questions.hint: Alexander Graham Bell is often associated with this.
questions.objective: Identify important inventions that changed communication.

questions.q: What is the name of our home galaxy?
questions.explanation: The Milky Way is the galaxy that contains our Solar System.
questions.hint: It sounds like a popular candy bar.
questions.objective: Understand basic facts about the solar system and space.

questions.q: Which fruit is famous for keeping the doctor away if you eat one every day?
questions.explanation: There is a famous proverb that says, 'An apple a day keeps the doctor away.'
questions.hint: It can be red, green, or yellow.

questions.q: What is the largest ocean on Earth?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest and deepest of Earth's oceanic divisions.
questions.hint: It covers more than 30% of the Earth's surface.
questions.objective: Learn basic geography facts about oceans.

questions.q: Which type of weather usually involves lightning and thunder?
questions.explanation: Thunderstorms are storms that produce lightning and thunder.
questions.hint: It sounds like 'thunder' is in the name.

questions.q: What is the main purpose of wearing sunscreen?
options: To protect skin from the sun
questions.answer: To protect skin from the sun
questions.explanation: Sunscreen helps protect your skin from harmful UV rays that can cause sunburn.
questions.hint: Think about what you wear when you go to the beach.

questions.q: How many continents are there on Earth?
questions.explanation: The seven continents are Africa, Antarctica, Asia, Europe, North America, Australia, and South America.
questions.hint: It is the same number as the days in a week.

questions.q: Which animal is known as the 'King of the Jungle'?
questions.explanation: The lion is traditionally called the king of the jungle because of its status as an apex predator.
questions.hint: It has a large mane of hair around its neck.
questions.objective: Identify animals and their common associations.
